decision_reason: refactor capability + allowed_risk includes medium; mechanical DRY/SRP extraction confined to one file, no behavior change

packet.objective: Deduplicate AgentEvent emission + idempotency-key parsing in CliAgentRunner, remove duplicate classify_failure call, remove dead _terminate_process_group wrapper

decision_reason: refactor capability; splitting closures/helpers in one file with existing test coverage, keep on same reliable runtime as task A given low quota confidence

packet.objective: Extract shared cap_bytes validation between _BoundedLog/_BoundedBuffer and split supervise_process closures for SRP, no behavior change

command:
You are editing exactly one file: backend/src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py

Goal: mechanical SOLID/DRY cleanup only. NO behavior change. Do not touch any other file.

Make these four changes:

1. `CliAgentRunner.run()` currently constructs three separate `AgentEvent(...)` literals
   (for "agent.started", "agent.failed", and each "runtime.output"/"agent.finished" event),
   each repeating plan_id, goal_id, run_id, attempt_id, task_id, attempt. Extract a small
   private helper method, e.g.:

   ```python
   async def _emit(
       self,
       event_sink: AgentEventSink,
       *,
       plan_id: str,
       goal_id: str | None,
       run_id: str | None,
       attempt_id: str | None,
       task_id: str,
       attempt: int,
       seq: int,
       event_type: str,
       payload: dict[str, object],
   ) -> None:
       await event_sink.emit(
           AgentEvent(
               plan_id=plan_id, goal_id=goal_id, run_id=run_id, attempt_id=attempt_id,
               task_id=task_id, attempt=attempt, seq=seq, type=event_type, payload=payload,
           )
       )
   ```

   and call it from the three emission sites in `run()`, passing the same field values as
   before (no field renamed, no field dropped, no seq numbering changed).

2. `correlation_env()` and `run()` each independently do
   `parts = idempotency_key.split(":")` and check `len(parts) == 6` to derive
   plan_id/goal_id/task_id/run_id/attempt_number/attempt_id. Introduce one small helper
   (module-level function, e.g. `_parse_idempotency_key(idempotency_key: str) -> tuple[...] | None`
   or a small frozen dataclass) that both `correlation_env()` and `run()` call, so the
   6-field-vs-legacy-3-field parsing logic exists in exactly one place. Preserve exact
   existing behavior: `correlation_env` still returns `{}` for non-6-part keys; `run()`
   still falls back to `goal_id=None, run_id=None, attempt_id=None, attempt_number=task.attempt`
   for non-6-part keys.

3. In `_run_sync`, `classify_failure(output)` is called directly right before calling
   `normalize_failure(stdout=stdout, stderr=stderr, ...)`, which internally computes the
   exact same `f"{stdout}\n{stderr}"` and calls `classify_failure` again. Remove the
   redundant direct call and its `output = f"{stdout}\n{stderr}"` line; get the kind from
   `failure.kind` (the `RuntimeFailure` returned by `normalize_failure`) instead, and use
   that in the `log.warning(..., kind=kind.value)` call.

4. Remove the `_terminate_process_group` instance method on `CliAgentRunner` (it is an
   unused one-line wrapper around the module-level `terminate_process_group` — confirmed via
   `grep -rn _terminate_process_group backend/src backend/tests` finding only its own
   definition). If the `terminate_process_group` import becomes unused as a result, remove
   that import too — but check first, since `supervise_process` (in process_supervisor.py,
   which you are NOT editing) already imports and uses it independently.

After editing, run from the repo root:
  cd backend && python -m ruff check src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py --fix
  cd backend && python -m mypy src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py
  cd backend && python -m pytest tests/unit/runtime/ -m "not integration" -q

All three must pass with zero errors before you finish. Do not modify test files — if a test
fails, you likely changed behavior; fix your refactor instead of the test.

  cd backend && python -m mypy src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py
  cd backend && python -m pytest tests/unit/runtime/ -m "not integration" -q

You are editing exactly one file: backend/src/infra/runtime/process_supervisor.py

Goal: mechanical SOLID/DRY cleanup only. NO behavior change. Do not touch any other file.

Make these two changes:

1. `_BoundedLog.__init__` and `_BoundedBuffer.__init__` each contain the identical guard:
   `if cap_bytes < 256: raise ValueError("...")` (with a slightly different message string
   in each — "log cap must be at least 256 bytes" vs "buffer cap must be at least 256
   bytes"). Factor this into one small module-level helper, e.g.:

   ```python
   def _require_min_cap(cap_bytes: int, *, label: str) -> None:
       if cap_bytes < 256:
           raise ValueError(f"{label} cap must be at least 256 bytes")
   ```

   and call it from both constructors with their respective label ("log" / "buffer"), so the
   exact same error messages are still raised for the same inputs. Do NOT merge `_BoundedLog`
   and `_BoundedBuffer` into one class — they have different storage semantics (durable JSONL
   file vs in-memory tail) and must stay separate.

   Also, `_BoundedLog.write` currently mixes JSON-record serialization, file I/O, and the
   truncation/marker byte-math (computing `budget`, `tail`, finding the newline cut, building
   `retained`) all in one method. Extract just the truncation-math part (given existing file
   bytes, the new record, cap_bytes, and the marker, compute the bytes to retain) into a small
   private helper method, e.g. `_compute_retained(self, existing: bytes, record: bytes) ->
   bytes`, called from `write()`. Keep the exact same byte-level algorithm — same budget
   calculation, same newline-cut logic, same fallback when budget == 0 — just moved into its
   own method so `write()` only does: build the record, decide whether to truncate, do the
   file I/O.

2. `supervise_process` currently has the `observe(...)` closure and the `read_stream(...)`
   closure defined inline inside the function body (roughly 100 lines total covering process
   spawning, thread lifecycle, timeout/kill orchestration, observation building, and stream
   reading in one function). Extract `observe` into a small private module-level or nested
   helper that takes the same closed-over values as explicit parameters instead of relying on
   closure capture, e.g. a `_ObservationEmitter` small class or a plain function
   `_emit_observation(on_observation, kind, payload, *, plan_id, goal_id, task_id, run_id,
   attempt_id, attempt_number)` — pick whichever keeps `supervise_process` shortest and
   clearest. Likewise extract `read_stream` into a standalone function/method that takes
   `buffers`, `log`, `on_output` as explicit parameters rather than closure capture. The goal
   is that `supervise_process`'s body reads as: build log+buffers, spawn process, start
   threads (calling the extracted read function), wait/timeout/terminate, join threads, emit
   final observation (calling the extracted emit function), build and return the result — each
   step delegated, not inlined. Do NOT change the order of operations, the timeout/termination
   logic, or the `finally` block's behavior (including its existing behavior on the
   non-timeout path — a correctness question is out of scope here, only reorganize into
   testable pieces without changing what runs, when).

After editing, run from the repo root:
  cd backend && python -m ruff check src/infra/runtime/process_supervisor.py --fix
  cd backend && python -m mypy src/infra/runtime/process_supervisor.py
  cd backend && python -m pytest tests/unit/runtime/ -m "not integration" -q

All three must pass with zero errors before you finish. Do not modify test files — if a test
fails, you likely changed behavior; fix your refactor instead of the test.
